name: Graphic Design
description: Supporting graphic devices layered on top of colour, type, and imagery: shape/corner language, photo overlay treatments, the logomark used as a background watermark, and seasonal campaign badges.
keyRule: Rounded corners are the default shape language for photography and containers; the correct/avoid example specifically calls out over-rounding (corners rounded past what suits the image's own content) as the failure mode, not squareness itself.

pages.name: Shapes
pages.description: States rounded corners as the standard treatment for photography and shape elements, with a correct-vs-avoid example pair showing an over-rounded corner as the incorrect case.

pages.name: Shapes (applied)
pages.description: Applied examples on real vehicle/logistics photography showing the rounded-corner treatment combined with colour blocking and caption cards (e.g. 'Boosted delivery times', 'Improved driver safety').

pages.name: Overlay elements
pages.description: The orange-to-black and blue gradient treatments (see Colour.doc.json → Gradients) applied specifically as photo overlays, plus textured/pattern overlay variants for section backgrounds.

pages.name: Logomark
pages.description: Rules for using the arrow mark as a large, low-opacity background watermark/graphic device — distinct from the Logo section's rules for the mark used as identification. Includes seasonal badge executions (Spring, Heritage Day, Christmas) that adapt the watermark treatment for campaign material.

doThis: Use rounded corners consistently on photography and containers, scaled to suit the image.
Use the approved overlay gradients rather than inventing new ones.
Use the logomark watermark treatment for campaign/seasonal material rather than a literal logo lockup.
dontDoThis: Don't over-round corners past what the image content supports.
Don't introduce new overlay/gradient directions outside Colour → Gradients.
Don't confuse the watermark use of the logomark with the identification use documented in Logo.doc.json → Logomark usage — they follow different rules (opacity/scale vs. clearspace/minimum size).
relationToProductTokens: Loosely relevant to primitive.radius in tokens.json (product uses a 4px MUI default radius). The brand's rounded-corner treatment on photography is a much larger radius applied to imagery/marketing containers, not a UI component radius — don't conflate the two scales.
openQuestions: Confirm the actual corner-radius value(s) used here if this treatment needs to be reproduced outside Figma (e.g. in a marketing site template).
